mission.title: Install and Configure Open Cluster Management on Kubernetes
mission.description: Open Cluster Management (OCM) is a CNCF sandbox project aimed at simplifying the management of heterogeneous, multicluster environments for Kubernetes applications. By installing OCM, you can effectively manage multicluster and multicloud scenarios, enhancing your Kubernetes experience.

steps.title: Add the OCM Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official OCM Helm repository to your Helm client to access the OCM charts.
```bash
helm repo add open-cluster-management https://open-cluster-management.github.io/manifests
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the OCM components
steps.description:
Install the OCM components using Helm, specifying the namespace for the installation.
```bash
helm install ocm open-cluster-management/cluster-manager --namespace ocm-system --create-namespace --version v1.2.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the OCM pods are running successfully in the ocm-system namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n ocm-system
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the OCM components to ensure optimal performance.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment ocm --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi -n ocm-system
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access the OCM API
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the OCM API service to access it locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/ocm-api -n ocm-system 8080:80
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the OCM Helm release to remove the deployed components.
```bash
helm uninstall ocm --namespace ocm-system
```

uninstall.title: Delete the OCM namespace
uninstall.description:
Clean up the namespace created for OCM to remove any remaining resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace ocm-system
```

uninstall.title: Remove Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
uninstall.description:
Delete any CRDs that were created during the installation of OCM.
```bash
kubectl delete crd clustermanagers.open-cluster-management.io
kubectl delete crd placementdecisions.open-cluster-management.io
kubectl delete crd placements.open-cluster-management.io
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and resources.
```bash
kubectl get all -n ocm-system -o yaml > ocm-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the OCM components
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the OCM components to the latest version available in the Helm repository.
```bash
helm upgrade ocm open-cluster-management/cluster-manager --namespace ocm-system --version v1.2.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the OCM pods are running the upgraded version successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n ocm-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your OCM pods are stuck in a Pending state, check for insufficient resources in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n ocm-system
```

troubleshooting.title: API service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the OCM API, ensure that the port-forward command is running and check the service status.
```bash
kubectl get svc -n ocm-system
```

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff errors
troubleshooting.description:
If you see CrashLoopBackOff errors, check the logs of the affected pod for more details.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n ocm-system
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of OCM will have all pods running in the ocm-system namespace without errors. You should be able to access the OCM API through the port-forwarded service.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add open-cluster-management https://open-cluster-management.github.io/manifests
helm repo update
helm install ocm open-cluster-management/cluster-manager --namespace ocm-system --create-namespace --version v1.2.0
kubectl set resources deployment ocm --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi -n ocm-system

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed.
